title: keckiella
intro: Keckiella, a genus of flowering plants in the mint family, is a type of shrubby perennial native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various health issues. The health benefits of Keckiella include reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and alleviating symptoms of respiratory infections. Keckiella is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and its essential oils are extracted for use in aromatherapy and herbal remedies.
